page: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server configured with 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system features 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, it includes two NVIDIA L40S GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering a combined 96 GB of VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance type, and it includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations indicate top-tier single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression all placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations also perform in the top 25%. In LLM inference testing, the server achieves top-tier speeds for prompt processing and text generation on small 135M models, and strong performance for text generation on medium 7B models, though prompt processing for 7B and 70B models falls into the average tier.

Given the inclusion of dual NVIDIA L40S GPUs and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 12 GB per core, this server offers a resource-dense profile for workloads requiring parallel processing and substantial memory capacity. The lack of local storage requires external storage integration, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This configuration is highly suited for machine learning workloads, including LLM inference on small to medium models, data science tasks, ray tracing, and database operations.
description: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server featuring an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 16 vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 192.0 GB of RAM. It is equipped with two NVIDIA L40S GPUs from the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing 96 GB of total VRAM. The server does not include local storage. In benchmark testing, it demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, ranking in the top 10% for single-threaded compression and Geekbench single-core metrics. Large-block memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression also rank in the top 25%. This resource-dense configuration is well-suited for parallel computing tasks, including small-to-medium LLM inference, database operations, and data science workloads.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and two NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB of VRAM, optimized for machine learning and parallel computing.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-2xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA L40S GPUs with 96 GB VRAM.
tagline: A dual-GPU accelerated server with high memory density and top-tier single-core performance for parallel processing and machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA L40S Ada Lovelace GPUs with 96 GB total VRAM
16 AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs with 192 GB of system memory
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Strong memory bandwidth and multi-core processing capabilities
Optimized for LLM inference, data science, and database operations
No local storage included, requiring external storage solutions
